preFix.title: Apothecary soy candle with herbs
preFix.subtitle: A clear glass jar soy candle with dried herb and wood layers and an earthy essential oil blend
preFix.excerpt: Layered dried herbs, bark, and flowers pressed against the inside of a tall apothecary jar give this soy candle the look of a specimen display. Scented with rosemary, thyme, and clary sage essential oils. The layers are decorative only and positioned safely away from the wax pool.
preFix.sourceNotes: Soy container wax pour temperature and fragrance load: Candlescience technical reference (2022). Essential oil blend (rosemary, thyme, clary sage) at 6%: IFRA guidelines for rinse-off and burn products. Botanical decoration safety in candles: botanicals fixed to inside glass wall are decorative and remain outside the wax pool during burning.

content.text: This candle uses a tall apothecary-style glass jar to show off layers of dried herbs and botanicals fixed against the inside of the glass. The arrangement is entirely decorative; the botanicals sit against the wall above the wax level and are never in contact with the burning wax pool. The essential oil blend of rosemary, thyme, and clary sage produces an earthy, herbal scent that suits the look of the jar.

items.name: Thyme essential oil
items.prepNote: 1% of wax weight; add last as thyme EO is potent

items.name: Dried herbs (assorted)
items.prepNote: fully dried rosemary sprigs, dried lavender, bay leaves, or similar

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: ECO-6 or CD-14 for a 6 to 8 cm jar diameter

content.text: Melt wax over a water bath only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach. Position all dried herbs above the wax fill line when pouring, clear of the flame and wax pool.

Warm the jar with a hairdryer. Fix the botanical layers to the inside of the jar above the fill line using small dabs of melted wax. Lay each piece flat against the glass and hold until the wax dab sets. Arrange them in bands or at random, keeping all material at least 1 cm above the planned wax fill level.
Melt the soy wax to 75 degrees C. Cool to 60 to 65 degrees C and stir in all three essential oils. Mix for 2 minutes to fully disperse the fragrance.
Centre the pre-tabbed wick with a wick bar. Pour the wax at the
of 60 to 65 degrees C, stopping at least 1 cm below the lowest herb. Leave undisturbed for 8 hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Follow the

rule: burn on the first light until the wax pool reaches the jar's full diameter. Subsequent burns should not reach the botanical layer above; if the wax level rises towards the herbs after several burns, extinguish and set the candle aside.

Store away from direct light and heat. The candle keeps several years. The essential oil fragrance is strongest in the first year. Discard when the wax is low enough that the jar warms significantly during use.

initialFindings.message: paragraph reads at grade 11.9 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
